name: Bundeswehr — German Armed Forces
detail: 181,500 active personnel (2024); includes Heer (army), Marine (navy), Luftwaffe (air force), Cyber Command. Underfunded for decades post-Cold War; Zeitenwende 2022 launched €100B Sondervermögen for rearmament. Target: 203,000 by 2031.

name: NATO Nuclear Sharing — Büchel Air Base
detail: ~20 US B61 gravity bombs deployed at Luftwaffe Büchel Air Base (Rhineland-Palatinate) under NATO nuclear sharing arrangement. German Tornado aircraft certified as delivery platforms; upgrade to F-35A under way. Existence officially unconfirmed per German policy.

name: Bundeswehr Panzer Units
detail: Germany operates Leopard 2 main battle tank (approx. 320 operational, 200+ in storage). Donated approx. 18 Leopard 2A6 tanks to Ukraine (2023). First joint NATO tank battalion with Netherlands (2016). Readiness concerns documented in Wehrbeauftragter annual reports 2014–2022.

name: Luftwaffe — Eurofighter Typhoon Fleet
detail: Germany operates ~93 Eurofighter Typhoons as primary combat aircraft. F-35A procurement announced 2022 as Tornado replacement for nuclear sharing mission. Major readiness issues documented pre-2022 (peak: 4 of 128 Eurofighters operational in 2018).

name: Bismarck Battleship (1939–1941)
detail: German battleship Bismarck: 50,300 tons displacement, largest Kriegsmarine vessel. Sank British HMS Hood (May 24, 1941); hunted by Royal Navy and sunk May 27, 1941 in North Atlantic after 9 days. 2,200 of 2,221 crew killed. Symbol of WWII German naval power.

name: Kriegsmarine U-Boat Fleet (WWII)
detail: Germany deployed over 1,100 U-boats in WWII; sank ~2,700 Allied ships (14.5M tons). Battle of the Atlantic turned decisively against Germany by mid-1943 (ENIGMA decoded, radar, long-range aircraft). Of 40,000 U-boat crew, ~28,000 (70%) died — highest loss rate of any WWII service branch.

name: Ramstein Air Base (NATO/USAF)
detail: Largest US Air Force base outside US, located in Rhineland-Palatinate. HQ of US Air Forces in Europe (USAFE) and NATO Allied Air Command. Ukraine Support Coordination Group (Ramstein Format) meets monthly since April 2022. Key hub for weapons deliveries to Ukraine.

name: Royal Prussian Army (18th–19th Century)
detail: Frederick the Great's army (1740–1786): 80,000 troops with strict discipline, oblique order tactics. Defeated larger Austrian/French forces at Rossbach (1757) and Leuthen (1757). Defeated by Napoleon at Jena-Auerstedt (1806) then reformed by Scharnhorst/Gneisenau. Template for German military excellence mythology.
